content.problemStatement:
Chef is given two arrays AAA and BBB, each having NNN elements.

In one move, Chef can choose an index iii (1≤i≤N−1(1 \leq i \leq N-1(1≤i≤N−1), get (Ai−Ai+1)(A_i - A_{i+1})(Ai​−Ai+1​) points, and then swap AiA_iAi​ and Ai+1A_{i+1}Ai+1​.

**For example:** If Chef has the array - [10,7,5][10, 7,5][10,7,5] and Chef chooses index 111 during his move, he will get 10−7=310 - 7 = 310−7=3 points and the new array will become [7,10,5][7 , 10 ,5][7,10,5]

Can you help Chef in finding the maximum number of points he can get while converting the array AAA into array BBB?

Note:\textbf{Note:}Note: It is guaranteed in the input that there exists a sequence of moves which can convert array AAA into BBB.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of testcases. The description of the TTT testcases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN denoting the number of elements in AAA and BBB.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space separated integers A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2,..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​.
The third line of each test case contains NNN space separated integers B1,B2,...,BNB_1, B_2,..., B_NB1​,B2​,...,BN​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer, which is the maximum number of points that Chef can get while converting the array AAA into array BBB
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: Chef can choose i=1i = 1i=1 in the first move, get 1−2=−11 - 2 = -11−2=−1 points and swap A1A_1A1​ and A2A_2A2​. After this move, AAA is converted into BBB. We can prove that there exists no other sequence of moves which can result in higher score while converting AAA into BBB.
Test Case 222: One possible sequence of moves is the following:

Choose i=2i = 2i=2. Total points =1= 1=1, and AAA will become [3,1,2][3, 1, 2][3,1,2].
Choose i=1i = 1i=1. Total points =1+2= 1 + 2=1+2, and AAA will become [1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2].
Choose i=2i = 2i=2. Total points =1+2+1= 1 + 2 + 1=1+2+1, and AAA will become [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3].

There are other possible sequences of moves which can result in total 444 points, but no such sequence exists which can result in more than 444 points, and simultaneously convert AAA into BBB.
